description_long: This is a potted flowering plant with slender green stems rising from a round, reddish-brown terracotta pot filled with dark soil. The plant features several delicate, nodding flowers with elongated, spurred petals in shades of red and yellow, resembling the distinctive shape of columbine flowers. The flowers are arranged at varying heights along the stems, each with a prominent central cluster of reproductive structures. The leaves are green, palmate, and deeply lobed, distributed sparsely along the stems. The pot has a slightly weathered, matte finish, and the overall plant appears healthy and upright. This object would commonly be found as decorative vegetation in urban parks, gardens, or on patios.
description: A potted flowering plant with red and yellow nodding flowers and palmate green leaves in a terracotta pot. The flowers have elongated, spurred petals and the plant stands upright with multiple stems.
description_view_0: Front view showing the terracotta pot, upright stems, and several nodding red and yellow flowers with palmate leaves.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the cluster of flowers and the distribution of leaves along the stems, with the pot's rim clearly visible.
description_view_2: Side view emphasizing the tallest flower and the spread of the stems, with fewer leaves visible from this angle.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back of the flowers and more of the pot's surface, with the stems and leaves partially obscured.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged radially around the center of each flower, with elongated spurs extending backward and some overlapping at the base, creating a whorled and symmetrical appearance typical of columbine flowers.
